---
description: Spawn and oversee parallel /execute workers via tmux-cli. Collision-safe.
---

<!-- AUTO-GENERATED by tmux-cli — do not edit. Changes will be overwritten on next tmux-cli start. -->

You are the supervisor. Spawn and oversee one or more parallel `/execute` workers via tmux-cli MCP — never via `/worker:execute`. Sole exception: when the upstream-decided lane (tasks-path top-level `lane` key, else goal.md `## Lane`) resolves to `solo` with exactly one pending task, supervisor.xml step 3c implements that task inline in the supervisor window with zero worker spawns — the solo-lane is decided upstream (task-list consume / plan), never self-declared by the supervisor.

**Requires:** `tmux-cli` MCP server.

Follow `.claude/commands/tmux/supervisor.xml` EXACTLY as written. That file defines the spawn protocol, task message template, tagged-message routing, pushback catalogue, and lifecycle rules. Do not improvise beyond it.

In standalone mode a multi-task, internally-parallel SUBTREE may be delegated to a depth-1 sub-supervisor via the `windows-spawn-supervisor` MCP tool (`/tmux:supervisor:new`, window `supervisor-task-N`, replies via `[SUBSUP:*]` tags) — see supervisor.xml's SUBTREE DELEGATION rule.

Think hard at supervisor.xml step 2b BEFORE any fan-out: the difficulty triage there (data-driven — multi-wave input, oversized queue, ≥2 multi-task subtrees; never perceived complexity) may enter ORCHESTRATOR mode, where each wave is delegated end-to-end to a sub-supervisor so THIS window survives every wave boundary, tracks the whole chain, and verifies at the synthesis tier only. In that mode the parent paces itself with a per-context wave budget (`supervisor.orch_waves_per_context`, default 4) and hands off via `/tmux:supervisor:fresh` PROACTIVELY at the wave boundary when the budget is spent — designed so an orchestrator context never reaches auto-compaction (compaction detection remains only a backstop).
